canonical_label: Conte, R. & Musette, M. (2008) The Painlevé Handbook (1st edition). Springer Netherlands, Dordrecht.
resolution_method: Crossref API direct DOI lookup of 10.1007/978-1-4020-8491-1 + 10.1007/978-3-030-53340-3 to verify edition disambiguation

primary_record_1st_edition_2008.title: The Painlevé Handbook
primary_record_1st_edition_2008.verification_evidence: Crossref returned matching title 'The Painlevé Handbook', authors {Conte, Musette}, Springer Netherlands 2008, with electronic ISBN 9781402084911 corresponding to the 1st edition print release

alternative_record_2nd_edition_2020.title: The Painlevé Handbook
alternative_record_2nd_edition_2020.edition_note: 2nd edition; expanded ~1.5x relative to 1st (1st edition has ~250 pp; 2nd has ~410 pp). Newer chapters cover discrete Painlevé + multi-component reductions; chart-map material in ch. 5/7 of 1st ed retained and updated.
alternative_record_2nd_edition_2020.cross_reference_to_086_envelope: T2-R5-LIT-HUNT-TRIANGULATION-086 used the 2nd edition as V5 (DOI 10.1007/978-3-030-53340-3); 069r1 cite 'Conte-Musette 2008' refers to the 1st edition specifically by year.

discrepancies_with_069r1_cite.actual_finding: 069r1's '2008' year-stamp uniquely identifies the 1st edition (DOI 10.1007/978-1-4020-8491-1, Springer Netherlands). However, the 2nd edition (2020, DOI 10.1007/978-3-030-53340-3, Springer International) contains expanded coverage and is the version referenced in T2-R5-LIT-HUNT-TRIANGULATION-086.
discrepancies_with_069r1_cite.recommendation: If 069r1 substrate path requires the 2nd edition's expanded material on discrete Painlevé or multi-component reductions, synth should switch to 2nd ed. Otherwise the 1st ed (069r1's literal cite) is sufficient.

accessibility_tier_definition: Tier 3 = paywalled with no open-access preprint; institutional ILL or purchase required

accessibility_evidence.1st_edition_2008: Springer Netherlands subscription/purchase required via link.springer.com/book/10.1007/978-1-4020-8491-1; purchase price typically USD 80-130 for hardback or chapter-pay-per-view USD 30/chapter; institutional access via SpringerLink common at Japanese universities; print copy may be borrowable via Yokohama-area library ILL.
accessibility_evidence.2nd_edition_2020: Springer International subscription/purchase required via link.springer.com/book/10.1007/978-3-030-53340-3; purchase price typically USD 100-150; institutional access wider (more recent).
accessibility_evidence.open_access_alternative: None. Conte's personal website (https://crconte.pagesperso-orange.fr/) hosts a few related preprint chapters but not the full Handbook. Some chapter-level early drafts may be on HAL but require manual verification.

estimated_acquisition_cost_usd: 80-150 for full book purchase; $0 via institutional ILL; ~30/chapter for SpringerLink chapter pay-per-view

primary_substrate_gap_target.rationale: Conte-Musette 1st ed serves as analytic-guidance fallback (Path α) for 069r1. Ch. 5 provides the Painlevé-Kowalevski test framework needed to verify chart-map well-definedness; Ch. 7 covers the perturbative reductions that may close the (a_0,a_1,a_2)→(α_inf,α_0,β_inf,β_0) namespace mapping. Ch. 5 is the higher-priority chapter for the F1 substrate gap.
primary_substrate_gap_target.sufficiency_for_chart_map: Conte-Musette 1st ed alone is INSUFFICIENT to close the specific P_III τ-function bridge needed at A.1.5.F1 (Conte-Musette covers Painlevé property + general reductions but not the random-matrix τ-function characterization). Pairing with Forrester-Witte 2002 (10.1002/cpa.3021) closes the gap. Pairing with Jimbo-Miwa Part II (10.1016/0167-2789(81)90021-X) provides the original isomonodromic-deformation derivation.
